ocr.text: In Q1 2024, TSMC's wafer fabrication yield rates reflect a variety of metrics across different wafer types, specifically
categorized by wafer diameter—8 inch and 12 inch—resulting in significant financial and operational data. The SOI wafer type
achieved a units sold count of 50,000, with a yield rate of 95%, generating a revenue of 5M CNY against a cost of 200M CNY,
culminating in a production volume of 150M CNY and defect rates of 2%. In contrast, the 15M wafer type demonstrated robust
performance with units sold totaling 300M, a yield rate of 95.5%, and revenue reaching 20M CNY, while incurring a cost of
500M CNY, which indicates a production volume of 370M CNY and a defect rate of 1%. The 3M wafer type, particularly
focusing on GaN technology, recorded sales of 30,000 units with an impressive yield rate of 97%, generating revenue of 4M
CNY and a cost of 100M CNY, resulting in a production volume of 80M CNY and a defect rate of 0.5%. The overall production
volume of all wafer types combined reached a significant total of 170,000 CNY, with a total yield rate of 95.3% across the
board. The total revenue generated by TSMC's wafer fabrication efforts in this quarter amounts to 1.75B CNY, solidifying the
company's position within the semiconductor industry. The financial breakdown reveals a cost of 1B CNY incurred during this
period, which, when juxtaposed with the revenue figures, highlights the operational efficiency and effectiveness of TSMC's
production capabilities. The overall defect rates averaged 1%, underscoring a high-quality output consistent with industry
standards. Furthermore, the total production volume across various wafer types demonstrates TSMC's capability to meet
demand while maintaining high yield rates, which are critical for maximizing profitability in a competitive market. The data
indicates that TSMC is effectively managing its resources, as evidenced by the low defect rates and high yield percentages,
which are essential metrics for sustaining growth and innovation in semiconductor technology.
WaferType  UnitsSold  YieldRate  Revenue  Cost  ProductionVolume  DefectRates
WaferDiameter  8inch  12inch  Total  Q12024  CNY  CNY  CNY  CNY  CNY  CNY
200,000  95.8%  40M  1B  750M  3M  1%  TotalYield  95.3%  TotalRevenue  1.75B
As the company continues to innovate and expand its operational framework, the emphasis on yield rates, defect reduction, and
cost management will remain paramount to achieving future milestones and enhancing shareholder value. Overall, TSMC's
performance in Q1 2024 showcases a robust ability to produce high-quality semiconductor wafers, with financial metrics that
reflect both strong sales and effective cost management strategies. The combination of high yield rates across various wafer
types, coupled with strategic production volume planning, positions TSMC favorably in the Asia-Pacific semiconductor market,
ensuring continued relevance and competitiveness amid evolving industry dynamics. This quarter's figures not only highlight
TSMC's current operational success but also lay the groundwork for future advancements in semiconductor fabrication
technologies, as the company seeks to leverage its strengths to address the growing global demand for high-performance
electronics. In summary, TSMC's Q1 2024 results reflect a meticulous approach to wafer production and yield optimization,
reinforcing its status as a leader in the semiconductor landscape.
